channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trudeau says Canadian police went public with Indian diplomat allegations to prevent more violence
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Wednesday that Canada's national police force went public with its allegations against Indian diplomats this week because it had to disrupt violent acts in Canada including drive-by-shootings, extortions and even murder.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Italy expands its ban on surrogacy to overseas as critics say it targets same-sex couples
item.description: Italy on Wednesday criminalized citizens who go abroad to have children through surrogacy, while opponents slam it as “medieval” and discriminatory to same-sex couples.
item.dc:creator: Giada Zampano And Paolo Santalucia, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A Palestinian student was asleep in his tent at a hospital when an Israeli strike brought an inferno
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Shaban al-Dalu was sleeping in his tent in a central Gaza hospital's courtyard, still recuperating from wounds from an Israeli strike on a mosque a week earlier, when a new strike hit, setting off an inferno.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A look at the oldest stone tools ever discovered, dating back 3.3M years
item.description: The oldest stone tools ever discovered were unearthed in Kenya by a group of scientists at the Lomekwi 3 site. The stone tools unearthed are simple in their design and pre-date humans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Navy jet crashes in Washington state; crew members missing
item.description: A search is ongoing for a Navy aircraft and two crew members who crashed east of Mount Rainier in Washington state Tuesday, according to the Navy. The EA-18G Growler, based out of Naval Air Station Whidbey Island, Wash., crashed just after 3:20 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Zelensky unveils anticipated 'Victory Plan' for Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Wednesday publicly unveiled his “Victory Plan” for Ukraine’s defensive war against Russia, following closed-door pitches with world leaders and including the two U.S. presidential candidates.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Israel decides on possible Iran targets: 'Precise and deadly'
item.description: Israel reportedly has decided on which possible targets it could strike in Iran in response to an Oct. 1 barrage of missiles that Tehran fired at Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pressure grows for countries to deliver on promised biodiversity targets at UN conference
item.description: Two years after reaching a historic biodiversity agreement, countries will gather next week to determine whether they are making progress on efforts to save Earth's plant and animal life.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sen Cotton says Biden-Harris likely prolonged Gaza war, let aid go to terrorists: 'Betrayed' taxpayers
item.description: Amid Israel’s existential seven-front war to root out terrorists on its borders, Arkansas GOP Sen. Tom Cotton says U.S. humanitarian aid might have got into the hands of terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US warns of growing North Korean military support for Russia's war
item.description: U.S. Deputy Secretary of State Kurt Campbell says Washington and its allies are “alarmed” by North Korea’s nuclear and missile threats as well as its increasing military support for Russia’s war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China's leader makes rare visit to province facing Taiwan following major war exercises
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China’s President Xi Jinping made a rare visit to a province facing Taiwan following military exercises that mobilized China's navy, air force, missile force and land troops to simulate a blockade of the self-governing island democracy that Beijing claims as its own territory a
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: In the heartland of Mexico's Sinaloa cartel, the old ways have changed and violence rages
item.description: Cellphone chats have become death sentences in the continuing, bloody factional war inside the Sinaloa drug cartel.
item.dc:creator: Mark Stevenson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: SEE IT: Suspect discovered hiding in couch on police bodycam while serving arrest warrant
item.description: Bodycam footage from police in the UK show a game of hide-and-seek that ended poorly for one suspect being served an arrest warrant over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN says Yemen risks being dragged into Mideast conflict that could spiral out of control
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Yemen risks being dragged further into the military escalation in the Middle East that keeps intensifying and could spiral out of control, the U.N. special envoy for the Arab world’s poorest nation said Tuesday. Hans Grundberg told the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US 'concerned' about reports of North Korean soldiers fighting alongside Russia in Ukraine
item.description: The U.S. says it is "concerned" about reports of North Korean soldiers fighting alongside Russian troops in the war against Ukraine, calling it "a new level of desperation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican president acknowledges army killings of 3 civilians in a violent border city
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico’s new president acknowledged Tuesday that three civilians including a child died in two shootouts involving the military last week in a violent border city.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: All about National Archaeological Museum in Athens, one of the world’s most prominent museums of its kind
item.description: The National Archaeological Museum houses artifacts dating back thousands of years. The collections are full of sculptures, artworks and more for guests to explore.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Hong Kong zoo seeks answers after 9 monkeys die in 2 days
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Hong Kong's oldest zoo is seeking answers in a monkey medical mystery after nine animals died in two days, including three members of a critically endangered species.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Deadly Israel shooting ruled a terror attack
item.description: A police officer was killed and four others were injured when a terrorist opened fire near a highway in Ashdod, Israel, on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taliban-run media stops showing images of living beings in some Afghan provinces
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Taliban run-media have stopped showing images of living beings in some Afghan provinces to comply with morality laws, an official confirmed Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel reveals Hezbollah special forces terrorist 'bunker' located under home with weapons, motorcycles
item.description: Israel's military has uncovered a bunker it says Hezbollah's special forces unit was using to plan a cross-border attack on Israeli citizens.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China unveils its plans to turn its feats in space exploration into scientific advances
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China has made great strides in exploring space in recent years, rocketing astronauts to its own space station and bringing back rocks from the moon. Now it wants to turn those feats into scientific advances.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A pastry brought to Mexico by British miners is still popular after 200 years
item.description: REAL DEL MONTE, Mexico (AP) — Isabel Arriaga Lozano carefully fills a small pastry with a savory mix of meat, potatoes and chili pepper. She is crafting a “paste” (pronounced PAH-stay), a beloved Mexican snack with a rich history.
item.dc:creator: INDIA GRANT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Campaigning begins for Japan's parliamentary election
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Official campaigning for Japan's Oct. 27 parliamentary election began Tuesday with new Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba seeking a mandate for his policies and for reforms after the governing party’s political funds scandal.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strike in northern Lebanon kills at least 21 people
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit an apartment building in northern Lebanon on Monday, killing at least 21 people, according to the Lebanese Red Cross. The Israeli military did not immediately comment and the target was not clear.
item.dc:creator: YAHYA HABCHITI, KAREEM CHEHAYEB, and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli forces seize documents that reveal Hamas plan for more elaborate attacks: report
item.description: Hundreds of documents seized by Israeli forces reveal a broader ambition by Hamas to plot attacks against Israel in the years before Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada expels India's top diplomat and alleges wider diplomatic involvement in crimes
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada said it has identified India’s top diplomat in the country as a person of interest in the assassination of a Sikh activist there and expelled him and five other diplomats Monday, in an escalating dispute over the June 2023 killing and allegations of other crimes.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, AIJAZ HUSSAIN and SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. to send troops and air defense system to Israel
item.description: The Pentagon announced the U.S. military will send a Terminal High Altitude Defense battery and about 100 U.S. troops to Israel.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, WGN 9

item.title: A bus carrying university students crashes, killing 12 and injuring 33 in Egypt's northeast
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — A bus carrying university students crashed and overturned on a highway in northeastern Egypt Monday, killing 12 people and injuring 33 others, the health ministry said in a statement. Students from the Suez-based Galala University were on board.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea is preparing to destroy northern parts of inter-Korean roads, Seoul says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea said Monday it has detected signs that North Korea is preparing to destroy the northern parts of inter-Korean roads no longer in use, as the rivals are embroiled in soaring tensions over North Korea’s claim that South Korea flew drones over its territory.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea sends warning to South Korea, saying troops ready to strike if more drones appear
item.description: North Korea said its army units are prepared to launch strikes against South Korea if another drone carrying propaganda flyers flies into the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah drone attack in Israel kills multiple IDF soldiers, wounds dozens of people: reports
item.description: A drone strike in Binyamina, Israel, on Sunday killed multiple IDF soldiers and injured dozens of people, in an attack Hezbollah has claimed responsibility for.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Heavy Israeli bombardment in northern Gaza as UN peacekeepers in Lebanon are hit again
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Palestinians in northern Gaza described heavy Israeli bombardment Saturday in the hours after airstrikes killed at least 22 people, as Israel warned people there and in southern Lebanon to get out of the way of offensives against the Hamas and Hezbollah militant groups.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine cracks down on alleged draft dodgers
item.description: Ukrainian military recruitment officers raided restaurants, bars and a concert hall in Kyiv, checking military registration documents and detaining men who were not in compliance.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and ELISE MORTON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia launches 4th aerial attack in a week against Ukraine's grain-exporting Odesa region
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A nighttime Russian missile strike on Odesa killed at least four people including a 16-year-old girl, regional authorities said Friday, in the latest in a series of attacks this week on the southern Ukrainian region that are likely intended to disrupt the country’s grain exports
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: British YouTuber and rapper Yung Filly is charged with raping and choking a woman in Australia
item.description: PERTH, Australia (AP) — A British YouTuber and rapper known as Yung Filly has been charged with raping and choking a woman in a hotel room following an Australian music performance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels attack a Liberian-flagged ship in the Red Sea
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels attacked a Liberian-flagged chemical tanker on Thursday in the Red Sea, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation
